title: Isaiah 60
subtitle: Arise, Shine, for Your Light Has Come!

sections.header: Verses 1–14 — “Nations Will Come to Your Light”

heb.paragraph: "Arise, shine, for your light (ʾōr) has come, and the glory of the LORD rises upon you. See, darkness covers the earth and thick darkness is over the peoples, but the LORD rises upon you and his glory appears over you. Nations will come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your dawn" (vv.1–3). THE Epiphany text. Darkness covers the earth; light breaks over Zion; nations stream toward it. Camels from Midian and Ephah, gold and incense from Sheba (v.6) — Matthew 2:11 (the Magi bringing gold and frankincense) fulfils this. "Your gates will always stand open, they will never be shut, day or night" (v.11). The sons of those who oppressed you will bow at your feet (v.14). Revelation 21:24–26 develops vv.3, 5, 11 for the new Jerusalem.

notes.note: "Arise, shine! Nations will come to your light, kings to the brightness of your dawn." The Epiphany: light in darkness, nations drawn. The verbs are imperatives: get up, glow.
"Herds of camels will cover your land. They will bring gold and incense." Matt 2:11: the Magi bring gold and frankincense. The fulfilment is specific.
"Your gates will always stand open." Rev 21:25: "On no day will its gates ever be shut." Isaiah blueprint, Revelation fulfilment.
"The sons of your oppressors will come bowing. They will call you the City of the LORD." The reversal: persecutors become worshippers.

sections.header: Verses 15–22 — “The LORD Will Be Your Everlasting Light”

heb.paragraph: "The sun will no more be your light by day, nor will the brightness of the moon shine on you, for the LORD will be your everlasting (ʿōlām) light, and your God will be your glory" (v.19). Revelation 21:23: "The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light." Sun and moon replaced by divine glory. "Your days of sorrow will end" (v.20). "Then all your people will be righteous and they will possess the land forever" (v.21). "The least of you will become a thousand, the smallest a mighty nation. I am the LORD; in its time I will do this swiftly" (v.22).

notes.note: "The LORD will be your everlasting light. Your sun will never set again; your days of sorrow will end." Rev 21:23; 22:5 draw directly from this. No more sun needed because God IS the light.
"The least will become a thousand, the smallest a mighty nation. In its time I will do this swiftly." The Abraham-promise (Gen 12:2) fulfilled cosmically. Swift in God timing.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVHerds of camels will cover your land, young camels of Midian and Ephah. And all from Sheba will come, bearing gold and incense and proclaiming the praise of the Lord.ESVA multitude of camels shall cover you, the young camels of Midian and Ephah; all those from Sheba shall come. They shall bring gold and frankincense, and shall bring good news, the praises of the LORD.NIV“Lift up your eyes and look about you: All assemble and come to you; your sons come from afar, and your daughters are carried on the hip.ESVLift up your eyes all around, and see; they all gather together, they come to you; your sons shall come from afar, and your daughters shall be carried on the hip.

rec.text: ""Arise, shine! Nations will come to your light, kings to the brightness of your dawn." The Epiphany: light in darkness, nations drawn. The verbs are imperatives: get up, glow."

rows.label: Everlasting Light — 60:15–22
rows.text: "The LORD will be your EVERLASTING LIGHT" (Rev 21:23). Sun replaced. Sorrow ENDS. "The least becomes a THOUSAND."

hebtext.note: "Arise, shine, for your light (ʾōr) has come, and the glory of the LORD rises upon you. See, darkness covers the earth and thick darkness is over the peoples, but the LORD rises upon you and his glory appears over you. Nations will come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your dawn" (vv.1–3). THE Epiphany text. Darkness covers the earth; light breaks over Zion; nations stream toward it. Camels from Midian and Ephah, gold and incense from Sheba (v.6) — Matthew 2:11 (the Magi bringing gold and frankincense) fulfils this. "Your gates will always stand open, they will never be shut, day or night" (v.11). The sons of those who oppressed you will bow at your feet (v.14). Revelation 21:24–26 develops vv.3, 5, 11 for the new Jerusalem.

"The sun will no more be your light by day, nor will the brightness of the moon shine on you, for the LORD will be your everlasting (ʿōlām) light, and your God will be your glory" (v.19). Revelation 21:23: "The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light." Sun and moon replaced by divine glory. "Your days of sorrow will end" (v.20). "Then all your people will be righteous and they will possess the land forever" (v.21). "The least of you will become a thousand, the smallest a mighty nation. I am the LORD; in its time I will do this swiftly" (v.22).

themes.note: Prophecy AND Mission peak at 10: Epiphany text, Matt 2:11, Rev 21:23–25. Nations stream to the light. Gates never shut. Everlasting light replaces sun and moon.
